Who I Am

Turning Data into Decisions

A data analyst passionate about uncovering the story behind the numbers — from macroeconomic policy to machine learning.

My Approach

I believe in the power of data-driven decision making. My goal is not just to analyze data, but to craft narratives that drive action — whether that's informing credit strategy, identifying revenue drivers, or predicting outcomes with machine learning.

What I Bring

Rigorous academic training in Economics and Mathematics from IBA Karachi, combined with hands-on experience applying statistical models to real-world datasets.

Proficient in the full analytics pipeline — from data cleaning and EDA to regression modeling, time-series forecasting, and interactive dashboard development.

Skilled at communicating complex findings clearly to both technical teams and business stakeholders.

My Work

My Projects

A selection of data science & analytics projects spanning econometrics, machine learning, and interactive visualisation.

01
Econometrics · Time Series
The Impact of SME Financing Structure and Credit Composition on SME Growth

Analyzed SME financing trends using SARIMA & SARIMAX models on national-level data (2016–2024). Identified that structured credit (trade finance & public lending) drives SME growth, while borrower count alone has minimal impact. Delivered policy-relevant insights for financial decision-making.

View Project
02
Regression · Finance
Credit Card Spend Modeling & Revenue Driver Analysis

Built and evaluated four regression models on 5,000 credit card customers to identify key revenue drivers and validate predictive performance using an independent test set.

View Project
03
Panel Data · Policy Analysis
Investigating the Impact of Beer Tax on Traffic Fatalities

Panel data econometric analysis in R examining the relationship between beer taxation and traffic fatalities across U.S. states using Fixed and Random Effects models. Demonstrates applied econometrics, policy analysis, and regression modeling on real-world data.

View Project
04
Data Visualisation · Dashboard
Haunted Places in the U.S.

Interactive dashboard mapping reported haunted locations in the U.S., revealing hotspots and state-wise encounter patterns.

View Project
05
Classification · Machine Learning
Titanic Survival Prediction | Classification Modeling & ROC Analysis in R

Built and compared multiple classification models to predict passenger survival using Logistic Regression, LDA, QDA, Naive Bayes, and kNN. Achieved 79% accuracy and 0.88 AUC with strong interpretability of survival drivers.

View Project
06
Random Forest · Predictive Modeling
Predicting Life Expectancy Using Tree-Based Machine Learning Models

Built and compared Regression Trees, Bagging, and Random Forest models to predict life expectancy using global socio-economic data (2014–2015). Evaluated model performance using cross-validation and MSE, identifying key drivers such as adult mortality and GDP influencing life expectancy outcomes.

View Project
